title: Cyrtandra karykrous
taxonomy: Cyrtandra karykrous is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a member of the genus Cyrtandra, which consists of various species of plants found in tropical regions, particularly in Asia and the Pacific. Cyrtandra karykrous is native to Papua New Guinea and is often found growing in humid forests and mountainous regions. The plant is typically epiphytic, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces, and has adapted to thrive in shaded environments. Within the Gesneriaceae family, Cyrtandra karykrous is classified as an endemic species, meaning it is unique to a specific geographic region.

The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting cells from oxidative damage.
